title: indigofera dalzelliana
intro: Indigofera dalzelliana, also known as the Dalzell's indigo, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family. It is a woody climber native to the Western Ghats of India and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The plant is valued for its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving cough and cold, and alleviating digestive problems. Indigofera dalzelliana is often prepared as a decoction, powder, or oil, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and its extracts have also been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial activities.
